compiled_state.title: Local workflow simplification: remove enforcement hooks and shift to PR-based merge
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

AI agents running in Claude Code worktree sessions currently encounter commit-time friction from local enforcement hooks: test-gate-status recording hits the ~73s Bash timeout ceiling (requiring test-batched.sh workarounds), review-gate diff hash verification occasionally invalidates after formatting auto-fixes, and Layer 2 bypass detection adds latency to every Bash tool call. Human developers experience the same friction as pre-commit hooks block commits pending review-status and test-gate-status validation. With CI enforcement established by Epic 3aaa-0238 (staged CI pipeline with LLM review and GitHub Ruleset enforcement), these local hooks are redundant — CI independently guarantees that no unreviewed or untested code reaches main. Removing the local enforcement hooks eliminates the agent timeout workarounds, removes commit-time review and test gates, and simplifies the developer experience to fast-feedback-only local checks. The merge-to-main workflow must also transition from direct push to PR-based to comply with the Ruleset.

## Success Criteria

- The local pre-commit test gate hook (pre-commit-test-gate.sh), review gate hook (pre-commit-review-gate.sh), and their entries in .pre-commit-config.yaml are removed in lockstep (same commit)
- The PreToolUse hooks that enforce no-verify restrictions (review-gate.sh), review-status files, and test-gate-status files are removed — CI enforcement via GitHub Rulesets replaces their function
- The local commit workflow retains tests, lint, and format as non-blocking Bash steps within the workflow (run before PR creation for fast feedback; failures surface as warnings, not commit blockers)
- A revised merge-to-main.sh (or replacement script) implements the PR-based workflow: create PR via gh pr create, poll CI status via gh pr checks or gh run watch, merge via gh pr merge with auto flag, and update the local worktree to origin/main after merge
- The COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md is updated to reflect the simplified local workflow (no review gate, no test gate, no LLM review step, non-blocking fast-feedback only)
- Existing worktree sessions that began before the hook removal receive a clear error message (non-zero exit with actionable guidance) if they attempt the old merge-to-main.sh direct-push path, directing them to create a PR instead
- When a PR is created via the DSO commit workflow, commits include Jira-Ticket: <value> and DSO-Task: <value> git trailers. DSO-Task is populated from the active session's DSO task ID; Jira-Ticket is populated from that task's SYNC event jira_key field. Each trailer is omitted when its source value is unavailable (no active task, or task not synced to JIRA). Human reviewers can follow either trailer value to the corresponding ticket system.

## Dependencies

- 3aaa-0238 (CI-enforced quality gates) must be completed and verified — CI enforcement must be operational before local hooks are removed, to avoid a window without enforcement
- 170b-2ac9 (commit-level source attribution via git-native trailers) — soft compatibility dependency for SC7; Jira-Ticket trailer infrastructure is delivered by that epic. SC7 can be partially satisfied by DSO-Task trailer alone if 170b-2ac9 is not yet shipped.

## Approach

Sequential migration: verify Epic A CI enforcement is green for at least one full PR cycle, then remove local enforcement hooks and rewrite merge-to-main.sh for PR-based workflow. Hook scripts and .pre-commit-config.yaml entries are removed in the same commit to avoid hook-not-found errors. The commit workflow (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md) is simplified to: run tests/lint/format for feedback, commit, push, create PR, wait for CI, merge. PreToolUse hooks (review-gate.sh) and associated artifact files (review-status, test-gate-status, reviewer-findings.json) are removed. Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task trailers are appended via git interpret-trailers during commit, using active-task context and the SYNC event jira_key field.

## Scenario Analysis

- Hook removal residue in .pre-commit-config.yaml (high): hook scripts and config entries removed in lockstep (same commit)
- In-flight sessions break on cutover (high): existing merge-to-main.sh sessions get 403 after Ruleset activation; revised script detects and emits clear error directing to PR workflow
- PR merge race (high): agent merge call may fail if branch HEAD changes; merge workflow handles 422 with retry-after-repoll
- gh pr merge auto edge cases (high): documented failure modes in cli/cli issues 8206, 8514; merge workflow implements explicit error detection and retry
- No active DSO task at commit time (high): Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task trailers omitted gracefully; commit proceeds without them
- Task not JIRA-synced (high): Jira-Ticket trailer omitted; DSO-Task still appended when active task ID is available

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 6 scenarios surviving blue team filter
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none

comments.body: Split from brainstorm session that also produced 3aaa-0238. Depends on 3aaa-0238 being completed and verified before implementation begins.

comments.body: Adversarial review: 10 findings, 10 accepted, 0 rejected. Key findings: orphaned artifact producers (review-status, test-gate-status), bypass-sentinel still sourced by dispatcher, version_bump phase incompatible with PR merge, 15+ test files need deletion, end-session/sprint caller interface impact.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: Story map finalized with 4 stories covering SC1-SC7.

Walking skeleton:
  5428-bebd (P1) - Remove local enforcement hooks → unblocks all downstream
  8787-ac16 (P1) - PR-based merge path
  cab1-600f (P1) - Git trailer injection (Jira-Ticket + DSO-Task) [SC7]
  ed9c-a277 (P3) - Docs update (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md trailer behavior, workflow simplification)

Dependency chain: 5428-bebd → {8787-ac16, cab1-600f} → ed9c-a277

Escalation policy: Autonomous — agents proceed with best judgment, document assumptions.

SC7 implementation notes:
- Jira-Ticket: populated from .tickets-tracker/<task_id>/SYNC* jira_key field
- DSO-Task: populated from active session task ID
- Both trailers omitted (not errored) when source unavailable
- git interpret-trailers --trailer format; blank-line separator automatic
- Reference: _resolve_jira_key() in plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py
- Compatibility: key names aligned with 170b-2ac9

Related: 170b-2ac9 (commit-level source attribution, soft compatibility dependency for SC7)
comments.author: Test
